event: UFC Fight Night: Allen vs. Costa

items.fight_news: Allen's southpaw jab and lateral movement will heavily test Costa's explosive kicking game. Costa relies heavily on body kicks, which Allen typically defends well with his lead elbow.
If Costa cannot establish the kicking range, Allen's boxing combinations will dictate the pace. The output differential in the later rounds heavily favors Allen's economical striking style.
Choi's devastating straight right hand relies on opponents stepping into the pocket. Santos is a high-volume pressure striker, making this a highly volatile stylistic clash.
The critical metric is whether Santos can angle off the centerline before Choi finds his timing. If Santos enters in straight lines, he plays directly into Choi's primary counter weapon.

Wellmaker's grappling control time is his primary win condition. Diaz needs to keep his hips heavy and avoid being backed against the fence, where Wellmaker initiates his highest-percentage chain-wrestling sequences.
Bukauskas utilizes a heavy calf-kicking game to neutralize movement. Edwards must establish early forward pressure to crowd Bukauskas and take away the kicking distance.

Cuamba's striking defense relies heavily on a high guard, leaving his midsection exposed. Sopaj needs to invest in body work early to lower Cuamba's hands and open up head strikes in the later rounds.

Williams brings explosive one-punch knockout power, but Veretennikov's technical boxing and range management are designed to neutralize blitzes. The key tactical battle is Veretennikov's jab against Williams' looping overhands.

Erslan's power is concentrated heavily in the first round. Tokkos must utilize lateral movement and clinch defense to survive the early onslaught, dragging Erslan into deep waters where his output historically drops.

Minev is a late replacement, meaning his cardio for a full 15 minutes is a massive question mark. Gantt needs to force grappling scrambles early to tax Minev's gas tank rather than engaging in a pure kickboxing match.

Vieira's elite top control is the absolute deciding factor if this fight hits the mat. Cavalcanti must use her length to maintain distance and punish Vieira's takedown entries with intercepting knees and counter-hooks.

Both men are explosive wrestlers, but Petroski has shown better submission-chaining from top position. Brundage tends to rely on explosive slams, which can drain his energy heavily if he fails to secure dominant positions.

Viana's guard is one of the most dangerous in the strawweight division. Ardelean must prioritize posture and ground-and-pound over passing attempts to avoid Viana's rapid submission setups off her back.

Barez thrives in chaotic striking exchanges, while Gurule prefers a more measured, technical approach. Gurule needs to disrupt Barez's rhythm with defensive wrestling and clinch work to prevent the fight from turning into a pure brawl.

Bannon's volume striking is her best asset, but Caliari's power advantage is significant. Bannon must maintain a high output while strictly avoiding the pocket, where Caliari can land fight-ending counters.
